---
title: "Runtime, Memory, and Execution"
---

# Runtime, Memory, and Execution

Range: `0609-0684`.

## Completion

| Status   | Count |
| -------- | ----- |
| authored | 16    |
| stub     | 60    |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                      | Completion | Summary                                                                                                                                    |
| ----- | ----------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 0609  | `STUB` [address space](#rune-0609)        | stub       | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0610  | `STUB` [aliasing](#rune-0610)             | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0611  | [allocation](#rune-0611)                  | authored   | Reserve memory for something that is about to live.                                                                                        |
| 0612  | `STUB` [allocator](#rune-0612)            | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0613  | `STUB` [arena](#rune-0613)                | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0614  | `STUB` [borrow {Run}](#rune-0614)         | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0615  | [cache {Run}](#rune-0615)                 | authored   | Keep likely things close so future cost falls. Shadow: stale truth delivered at machine speed.                                             |
| 0616  | `STUB` [cache line {Run}](#rune-0616)     | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0617  | `STUB` [call frame](#rune-0617)           | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0618  | `STUB` [call stack](#rune-0618)           | stub       | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0619  | `STUB` [closure environment](#rune-0619)  | stub       | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0620  | `STUB` [compaction {Run}](#rune-0620)     | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0621  | [copy-on-write {Run}](#rune-0621)         | authored   | Share until mutation forces a real duplicate.                                                                                              |
| 0622  | `STUB` [core dump {Run}](#rune-0622)      | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0623  | `STUB` [deallocation](#rune-0623)         | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0624  | `STUB` [destructor {Run}](#rune-0624)     | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0625  | `STUB` [dynamic allocation](#rune-0625)   | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0626  | `STUB` [eager evaluation](#rune-0626)     | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0627  | `STUB` [escape analysis](#rune-0627)      | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0628  | `STUB` [execution context](#rune-0628)    | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0629  | `STUB` [finalizer](#rune-0629)            | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0630  | `STUB` [frame pointer](#rune-0630)        | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0631  | [garbage collection](#rune-0631)          | authored   | Automatic reclamation of memory no longer reachable.                                                                                       |
| 0632  | `STUB` [generation](#rune-0632)           | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0633  | `STUB` [handle {Run}](#rune-0633)         | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0634  | [heap](#rune-0634)                        | authored   | Region for dynamically allocated objects.                                                                                                  |
| 0635  | `STUB` [heap object](#rune-0635)          | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0636  | [hot path {Run}](#rune-0636)              | authored   | The path where small inefficiencies burn real time.                                                                                        |
| 0637  | `STUB` [inline cache](#rune-0637)         | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0638  | `STUB` [interpreter loop](#rune-0638)     | stub       | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0639  | [JIT](#rune-0639)                         | authored   | A just-in-time word; translation happens at runtime rather than earlier in the forge.                                                      |
| 0640  | `STUB` [layout {Run}](#rune-0640)         | stub       | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0641  | `STUB` [lazy evaluation](#rune-0641)      | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0642  | `STUB` [leak {Run}](#rune-0642)           | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0643  | [lifetime {Run}](#rune-0643)              | authored   | How long a value may legally remain alive.                                                                                                 |
| 0644  | `STUB` [live set](#rune-0644)             | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0645  | [locality](#rune-0645)                    | authored   | The performance dividend from keeping related data physically close.                                                                       |
| 0646  | `STUB` [lock-free {Run}](#rune-0646)      | stub       | A progress word; some thread always moves forward without a lock.                                                                          |
| 0647  | `STUB` [mark phase](#rune-0647)           | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0648  | `STUB` [memory {Run}](#rune-0648)         | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0649  | [memory barrier](#rune-0649)              | authored   | An ordering fence for memory effects across threads or cores.                                                                              |
| 0650  | `STUB` [memory map](#rune-0650)           | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0651  | `STUB` [memory pool](#rune-0651)          | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0652  | [memory safety](#rune-0652)               | authored   | Protection against illegal memory access and corruption.                                                                                   |
| 0653  | `STUB` [move semantics](#rune-0653)       | stub       | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0654  | `STUB` [object header](#rune-0654)        | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0655  | `STUB` [page {Run}](#rune-0655)           | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0656  | `STUB` [page fault](#rune-0656)           | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0657  | [pinning](#rune-0657)                     | authored   | Keep an object fixed in place so other machinery can safely refer to it.                                                                   |
| 0658  | `STUB` [pointer {Run}](#rune-0658)        | stub       | A location word; it does not hold the thing, but where the thing may be found.                                                             |
| 0659  | `STUB` [pool allocator](#rune-0659)       | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0660  | `STUB` [prefetch](#rune-0660)             | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0661  | `STUB` [quiescence](#rune-0661)           | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0662  | `STUB` [reclaim](#rune-0662)              | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0663  | [reference count](#rune-0663)             | authored   | Ownership tracked by counting how many references remain.                                                                                  |
| 0664  | `STUB` [register allocation](#rune-0664)  | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0665  | `STUB` [root set](#rune-0665)             | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0666  | [runtime](#rune-0666)                     | authored   | A living word; the conditions under which the program presently exists and executes.                                                       |
| 0667  | `STUB` [sandbox {Run}](#rune-0667)        | stub       | A confinement word; actions are permitted only within a restricted space.                                                                  |
| 0668  | `STUB` [shadow stack](#rune-0668)         | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0669  | `STUB` [slab allocator](#rune-0669)       | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0670  | [stack {Run}](#rune-0670)                 | authored   | Region for call frames and short-lived local state.                                                                                        |
| 0671  | `STUB` [stack frame](#rune-0671)          | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0672  | `STUB` [stop-the-world](#rune-0672)       | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0673  | `STUB` [sweep phase](#rune-0673)          | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0674  | `STUB` [tail call](#rune-0674)            | stub       | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0675  | `STUB` [thread-local storage](#rune-0675) | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0676  | `STUB` [TLB](#rune-0676)                  | stub       | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0677  | `STUB` [tracing collector](#rune-0677)    | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0678  | `STUB` [unbox](#rune-0678)                | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0679  | `STUB` [user heap](#rune-0679)            | stub       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0680  | `STUB` [virtual memory](#rune-0680)       | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0681  | `STUB` [VM](#rune-0681)                   | stub       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0682  | `STUB` [wait-free {Run}](#rune-0682)      | stub       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0683  | `STUB` [write barrier](#rune-0683)        | stub       | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0684  | [zero-copy {Run}](#rune-0684)             | authored   | Data movement stripped down to the minimum number of duplications. Use it when the bottleneck is physical movement rather than arithmetic. |

## Entries

### [0609] address space {#rune-0609}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0610] aliasing {#rune-0610}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0611] allocation {#rune-0611}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Reserve memory for something that is about to live.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** Reserve memory for something that is about to live.

### [0612] allocator {#rune-0612}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0613] arena {#rune-0613}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0614] borrow {Run} {#rune-0614}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0615] cache {Run} {#rune-0615}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Keep likely things close so future cost falls.

**Shadow:** stale truth delivered at machine speed.

**Pocket gloss:** Keep likely things close so repeated access costs less.

### [0616] cache line {Run} {#rune-0616}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0617] call frame {#rune-0617}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0618] call stack {#rune-0618}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0619] closure environment {#rune-0619}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0620] compaction {Run} {#rune-0620}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0621] copy-on-write {Run} {#rune-0621}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Share until mutation forces a real duplicate.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** Share until mutation forces a real duplicate.

### [0622] core dump {Run} {#rune-0622}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0623] deallocation {#rune-0623}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0624] destructor {Run} {#rune-0624}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0625] dynamic allocation {#rune-0625}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0626] eager evaluation {#rune-0626}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0627] escape analysis {#rune-0627}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0628] execution context {#rune-0628}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0629] finalizer {#rune-0629}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0630] frame pointer {#rune-0630}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0631] garbage collection {#rune-0631}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Automatic reclamation of memory no longer reachable.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** Automatic reclamation of memory no longer reachable.

### [0632] generation {#rune-0632}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0633] handle {Run} {#rune-0633}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0634] heap {#rune-0634}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Region for dynamically allocated objects.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** Region for dynamically allocated objects.

### [0635] heap object {#rune-0635}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0636] hot path {Run} {#rune-0636}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The path where small inefficiencies burn real time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** The path where small inefficiencies burn real time.

### [0637] inline cache {#rune-0637}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0638] interpreter loop {#rune-0638}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0639] JIT {#rune-0639}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A just-in-time word; translation happens at runtime rather than earlier in the forge.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0640] layout {Run} {#rune-0640}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0641] lazy evaluation {#rune-0641}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0642] leak {Run} {#rune-0642}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0643] lifetime {Run} {#rune-0643}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** How long a value may legally remain alive.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** How long a value may legally remain alive.

### [0644] live set {#rune-0644}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0645] locality {#rune-0645}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The performance dividend from keeping related data physically close.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** The performance dividend from keeping related data physically close.

### [0646] lock-free {Run} {#rune-0646}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A progress word; some thread always moves forward without a lock.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0647] mark phase {#rune-0647}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0648] memory {Run} {#rune-0648}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0649] memory barrier {#rune-0649}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** An ordering fence for memory effects across threads or cores.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** An ordering fence for memory effects across threads or cores.

### [0650] memory map {#rune-0650}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0651] memory pool {#rune-0651}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0652] memory safety {#rune-0652}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Protection against illegal memory access and corruption.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** Protection against illegal memory access and corruption.

### [0653] move semantics {#rune-0653}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0654] object header {#rune-0654}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0655] page {Run} {#rune-0655}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0656] page fault {#rune-0656}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0657] pinning {#rune-0657}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Keep an object fixed in place so other machinery can safely refer to it.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** Keep an object fixed in place so other machinery can safely refer to it.

### [0658] pointer {Run} {#rune-0658}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A location word; it does not hold the thing, but where the thing may be found.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0659] pool allocator {#rune-0659}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0660] prefetch {#rune-0660}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0661] quiescence {#rune-0661}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0662] reclaim {#rune-0662}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0663] reference count {#rune-0663}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Ownership tracked by counting how many references remain.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** Ownership tracked by counting how many references remain.

### [0664] register allocation {#rune-0664}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0665] root set {#rune-0665}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0666] runtime {#rune-0666}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A living word; the conditions under which the program presently exists and executes.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0667] sandbox {Run} {#rune-0667}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A confinement word; actions are permitted only within a restricted space.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0668] shadow stack {#rune-0668}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0669] slab allocator {#rune-0669}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0670] stack {Run} {#rune-0670}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Region for call frames and short-lived local state.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** Region for call frames and short-lived local state.

### [0671] stack frame {#rune-0671}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0672] stop-the-world {#rune-0672}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0673] sweep phase {#rune-0673}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0674] tail call {#rune-0674}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0675] thread-local storage {#rune-0675}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0676] TLB {#rune-0676}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0677] tracing collector {#rune-0677}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0678] unbox {#rune-0678}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0679] user heap {#rune-0679}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0680] virtual memory {#rune-0680}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0681] VM {#rune-0681}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0682] wait-free {Run} {#rune-0682}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0683] write barrier {#rune-0683}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

### [0684] zero-copy {Run} {#rune-0684}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Data movement stripped down to the minimum number of duplications. Use it when the bottleneck is physical movement rather than arithmetic.

**Shadow:** runtime convenience can hide allocation, lifetime, contention, or stale-state costs.

**Pocket gloss:** Move data without making redundant copies.
